100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On February 23, 1926, the League of Nations took a significant step towards maintaining global peace by discussing measures to curb the arms race among member nations. In the United States, the Senate continued heated debates over the administration's budget proposals, reflecting ongoing tensions in domestic politics. Meanwhile, in Europe, the political climate remained unstable as strikes and labor unrest persisted, particularly in France and Germany, raising concerns about economic recovery post-World War I.
Updated: Monday, February 23, 2026 at 06:31:27 AM CST
